common_axes.name: Goal coverage & decision usefulness
common_axes.definition: How well the source helps choose, compare, or judge a solution against the stated goal.

common_axes.name: Core idea strength
common_axes.definition: Quality and value of the central evaluation or build idea independent of writing polish.

common_axes.name: Technical soundness
common_axes.definition: Whether the reasoning, contracts, and technical mechanics are internally credible.

common_axes.name: Structural completeness
common_axes.definition: Whether essential workflow and artifact components are present.

common_axes.name: Operationalization detail
common_axes.definition: How executable the method is without hidden steps.

common_axes.name: Evidence traceability
common_axes.definition: How clearly conclusions and PASS claims resolve to observation-bearing evidence.

common_axes.name: Risk & control adequacy
common_axes.definition: How well the source prevents drift, ambiguity, false positives, and unfair scoring.

common_axes.name: Internal consistency
common_axes.definition: Whether the source avoids contradictions, overlaps, and self-undermining rules.

common_axes.name: Document discipline & terminology
common_axes.definition: Clarity, naming discipline, identity hygiene, and terminology consistency.

build_package.name: Goal and objective precision
build_package.definition: Goal, objectives, success boundary, and non-goals are explicit and testable.

build_package.name: Baseline and change-set control
build_package.definition: The authorized baseline, inherited contracts, intended changes, and preservation obligations are explicit.

build_package.name: Scope and file obligations
build_package.definition: Every required create/modify/delete action is grounded to governed artifacts and unauthorized scope is constrained.

build_package.name: Architecture and archetype fit
build_package.definition: Controls match the project archetype, dependencies, risk profile, and expected outputs.

build_package.name: Verification design
build_package.definition: Positive, negative, semantic, integration, replay, and regression checks are defined with explicit oracles.

build_package.name: Evidence contract
build_package.definition: Required evidence fields, provenance, observations, timestamps, commands, and hashes are specified.

build_package.name: Drift safeguards
build_package.definition: Semantic, contract, scope, identity, evidence, and validator drift are actively prevented or detected.

build_package.name: Oracle independence and defect sensitivity
build_package.definition: Release acceptance is not circular and includes mutation-sensitive or independently implemented checks.

build_package.name: Deliverable and acceptance clarity
build_package.definition: Deliverables, artifact boundaries, acceptance gates, and non-pass behavior are unambiguous.

agent_work.name: Authority acquisition
agent_work.definition: The agent demonstrably loaded and reconciled all controlling sources before planning.

agent_work.name: Plan and work grounding
agent_work.definition: Work is decomposed into traceable units grounded to requirements and concrete targets.

agent_work.name: Scope fidelity
agent_work.definition: Executed changes remain within authorized scope or use an explicit amendment path.

agent_work.name: Execution traceability
agent_work.definition: Commands, edits, intermediate states, failures, and corrections can be reconstructed.

agent_work.name: Evidence quality
agent_work.definition: Evidence contains observations rather than status assertions and resolves to the claims it supports.

agent_work.name: Test and validator quality
agent_work.definition: Checks use correct oracles, isolate defects, reject known-bad cases, and do not merely count coverage.

agent_work.name: Replay and final-artifact proof
agent_work.definition: Proof starts from the final candidate or clean extraction and is reproducible without hidden builder state.

agent_work.name: Correction-loop quality
agent_work.definition: Failures create focused corrective work and are revalidated without erasing evidence.

agent_work.name: Verdict discipline
agent_work.definition: Scores and verdicts are derived after gates and unresolved conditions are represented honestly.

build_result.name: Goal and requirement coverage
build_result.definition: The delivered result materially satisfies authorized goals and requirements.

build_result.name: Functional correctness
build_result.definition: Observable functions and declared behaviors operate correctly, including defined failure behavior.

build_result.name: Contract and schema integrity
build_result.definition: Machine contracts are strict enough for their purpose and all governed instances conform.

build_result.name: Semantic and data consistency
build_result.definition: Human and machine representations, data exports, terminology, and relationships remain synchronized.

build_result.name: Inventory, reference, and identity integrity
build_result.definition: Version identity, paths, inventories, hashes, references, and artifact boundaries are exact.

build_result.name: Evidence and release traceability
build_result.definition: Release claims resolve to current, observation-bearing evidence with clear provenance.

build_result.name: Reproducibility and replayability
build_result.definition: The result and required proof artifacts can be recreated from declared inputs in clean environments.

build_result.name: Validator and resilience quality
build_result.definition: Validation detects representative high-impact defects and cannot be bypassed by shallow conformance.

build_result.name: Baseline regression and drift
build_result.definition: Quality-relevant inherited contracts are preserved or intentionally superseded with evidence.

build_result.name: Packaging and document discipline
build_result.definition: The artifact is clean, navigable, internally consistent, and free from generated contamination or stale paths.

drift_dimensions.name: Goal drift
drift_dimensions.definition: The delivered or executed work no longer serves the controlling goal.

drift_dimensions.name: Scope drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Changes exceed or omit the authorized change set.

drift_dimensions.name: Semantic drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Meaning, terminology, relationships, or exported representations diverge.

drift_dimensions.name: Contract drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Schemas, APIs, invariants, or required constraints weaken or change unintentionally.

drift_dimensions.name: Structural drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Folders, artifact boundaries, inventories, or component topology diverge.

drift_dimensions.name: Evidence drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Evidence becomes stale, assertion-only, non-resolving, or detached from claims.

drift_dimensions.name: Validator drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Validation coverage or defect sensitivity no longer matches release claims.

drift_dimensions.name: Reproducibility drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Replay increasingly depends on hidden state, order, or external builder logic.

drift_dimensions.name: Dependency drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Dependencies, versions, or integration assumptions change without governed analysis.

drift_dimensions.name: Discipline drift
drift_dimensions.definition: Identity, paths, hashes, naming, or document hygiene degrade.

hard_gates.name: Source readability
hard_gates.fail_condition: A decisive required source cannot be read or identified.

hard_gates.name: False-PASS sensitivity
hard_gates.fail_condition: The release validator accepts representative critical mutations or no defect-sensitivity evidence exists for a high-risk release claim.

hard_gates.name: Observation-bearing evidence
hard_gates.fail_condition: Mandatory PASS claims are supported only by self-authored status assertions.

hard_gates.name: Final-artifact replay
hard_gates.fail_condition: A required runtime or bootstrap proof was not produced from the final candidate or a clean extraction.

hard_gates.name: Inventory and identity integrity
hard_gates.fail_condition: Authoritative inventory, identity, or release hashes materially disagree with the delivered artifact.

hard_gates.name: Critical contract regression
hard_gates.fail_condition: A critical inherited schema, API, invariant, or safety contract is weakened without explicit authorization and regression evidence.

hard_gates.name: Semantic synchronization
hard_gates.fail_condition: Authoritative semantic surfaces materially disagree and the conflict affects delivered meaning.

hard_gates.name: Controlled scope
hard_gates.fail_condition: Material unplanned work is introduced without an authorized amendment.

hard_gates.name: Verdict derivation
hard_gates.fail_condition: PASS or a perfect score is written before or independently of the required gate outcomes.

scoring.rounding: Round final stage and document scores to two decimal places; retain unrounded values during calculation.
scoring.unknown_rule: Do not replace missing evidence with a midpoint. Record null and reduce evidence coverage.
